name: Deep Debugging - Flask API
description: Debug a nested Flask API with bugs across authentication, database, and validation layers
system_prompt: You are an expert Python debugger. Use Read, StrReplace, Grep, and Shell to find and fix bugs. Test each fix.

virtual_fs./workspace/services/user_api/scripts/tes:
#!/bin/bash
# Test script that will fail due to bugs

# Test 1: Health check
curl -s http://localhost:5000/health

# Test 2: Create user without auth (should fail with 401)
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5000/users \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username": "john", "email": "john@test.com"}'

# Test 3: Create user with auth (will fail due to auth bug)
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5000/users \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: test-token-123' \
  -d '{"username": "john", "email": "john@test.com"}'

# Test 4: Get user (will fail due to type bug)
curl -s http://localhost:5000/users/1 \
  -H 'Authorization: test-token-123'

turns.content:
This nested Flask API under /workspace/services/user_api has multiple bugs causing failures.
1. Inspect the project structure first
2. Read app.py, auth middleware, and user_db files
3. Analyze scripts/test_api.sh to infer expected behavior
4. Fix all major issues in auth, db operations, and input validation

Do not assume flat files; navigate using tools.
